task_completion.description: Record 'Berylliosis' (ICD-10 J63.2) as an active condition for John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen in GNU Health showing the patient's conditions or disease profile with 'Berylliosis' or 'J63.2' listed as an active condition for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Document a clinical evaluation with Respiratory Rate >= 20 and Oxygen Saturation (osat) <= 94%.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clinical evaluation or vital signs screen in GNU Health for John Zenon showing Respiratory Rate (RR) of 20 or higher, and Oxygen Saturation (osat/O2) of 94% or lower.

task_completion.description: Prescribe a systemic corticosteroid (Prednisone, Methylprednisolone, or Dexamethasone).
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prescription or medication order screen in GNU Health for John Zenon showing a systemic corticosteroid such as Prednisone, Methylprednisolone, or Dexamethasone.
A lab test or diagnostic order screen in GNU Health showing at least 3 distinct tests ordered (e.g., CBC, Chest X-ray, Spirometry) for John Zenon.

task_completion.description: Schedule a follow-up appointment 14 to 30 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An appointment scheduling screen in GNU Health showing a follow-up appointment for John Zenon scheduled with a date 14 to 30 days from the current system date.

integrity.description: Agent used the GNU Health GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health graphical user interface being navigated and interacted with, not terminal commands or scripts making database changes.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual GNU Health application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The information should be entered into the appropriate fields within the GNU Health application windows, not typed into a text editor or a single generic text box.

integrity.description: Agent did not simply copy-paste the expected answers from the task description.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data should be properly distributed across the correct forms and fields (e.g., conditions, evaluations, prescriptions, lab orders, appointments) rather than pasted as a single block of text.
